name: SPC t 8
desc: highlight any character past the 80th column

name: SPC t f
desc: display the fill column (by default the fill column is set to 80)

name: SPC t h h
desc: toggle highlight of the current line

name: SPC t n n
desc: toggle line numbers as configured in .spacemacs

name: SPC t m c
desc: toggle the org task clock (available in org layer)

name: SPC t m n
desc: toggle the cat! (if colors layer is declared in your dotfile)

name: SPC t m s
desc: toggle system monitor (displayed in the minibuffer)

name: SPC t C-8
desc: global toggle highlight of characters for long lines

name: SPC b W
desc: go to workspace and window by buffer

name: SPC t k m
desc: show persistent major-mode keymap. Toggle off with SPC t k k

name: SPC t k t
desc: show persistent top-level keymap. Toggle off with SPC t k k

name: SPC h d l
desc: copy last pressed keys that you can paste in gitter chat

name: SPC h d s
desc: copy system information that you can paste in gitter chat

name: SPC h SPC
desc: discover Spacemacs documentation, layers and packages

name: SPC h i
desc: search in info pages with the symbol at point

name: SPC u SPC h I
desc: Open Spacemacs GitHub issue page with pre-filled information - include last pressed keys

name: SPC j 0
desc: go to the beginning of line (and set a mark at the previous location in the line)

name: SPC j $
desc: go to the end of line (and set a mark at the previous location in the line)

name: SPC j b
desc: go back to the previous location (before the jump)

name: SPC j d
desc: jump to a listing of the current directory

name: SPC j f
desc: jump to the definition of an Emacs Lisp function

name: SPC j i
desc: jump to a definition in buffer (imenu)

name: SPC j k
desc: jump to next line and indent it using auto-indent rules

name: SPC j u
desc: jump to a URL in the current buffer

name: SPC j v
desc: jump to the definition/declaration of an Emacs Lisp variable

name: SPC j n
desc: split the current line at point, insert a new line and auto-indent

name: SPC j o
desc: split the current line at point but let point on current line

name: SPC j s
desc: split a quoted string or s-expression in place

name: SPC w TAB
desc: switch to alternate window in the current frame (switch back and forth)

name: SPC w b
desc: force the focus back to the minibuffer

name: SPC w c c
desc: toggle visual centering of the current buffer

name: SPC w c .
desc: center buffer and enable centering transient state

name: SPC u SPC w d
desc: delete a window and its current buffer (does not delete the file)

name: SPC w t
desc: toggle window dedication (dedicated window cannot be reused by a mode)

name: SPC w h
desc: move to window on the left

name: SPC w l
desc: move to window on the right

name: SPC w m
desc: maximize/minimize a window (maximize is similar to delete-other-windows)

name: SPC w p m
desc: open messages buffer in a popup window

name: SPC w p p
desc: close the current sticky popup window

name: SPC w S
desc: horizontal split and focus new window

name: SPC w u
desc: undo window layout (used to effectively undo a closed window)

name: SPC w V
desc: vertical split and focus new window

name: SPC w x
desc: delete a window and its current buffer (does not delete the file)

name: SPC u SPC w 1
desc: make 1 window layout with force delete

name: SPC u SPC w 2
desc: make 2 windows layout (split vertically) with force delete

name: SPC u SPC w 3
desc: make 3 windows layout (split vertically) with force delete

name: SPC u SPC w 4
desc: make 4 windows layout (split in 2x2 grid) with force delete

name: SPC TAB
desc: switch to alternate buffer in the current window (switch back and forth)

name: SPC b d
desc: kill the current buffer (does not delete the visited file)

name: SPC u SPC b d
desc: kill the current buffer and window (does not delete the visited file)

name: SPC b e
desc: erase the content of the buffer (ask for confirmation)

name: SPC b n
desc: switch to next buffer avoiding buffers matching spacemacs-useless-buffers-regexp

name: SPC u SPC b m
desc: kill all buffers and windows except the current one

name: SPC b p
desc: switch to previous buffer avoiding buffers matching spacemacs-useless-buffers-regexp

name: SPC b R
desc: revert the current buffer (reload from disk)

name: SPC b s
desc: switch to the *scratch* buffer (create it if needed)

name: SPC b u
desc: reopen the most recently killed file buffer

name: SPC b x
desc: kill the current buffer and window (does not delete the visited file)

name: SPC b Y
desc: copy whole buffer to clipboard (useful when copying to a browser)

name: SPC b N h
desc: create new empty buffer in a new window on the left

name: SPC b N C-i
desc: create an indirect buffer from an existing opened buffer

name: SPC b N i
desc: create an indirect buffer that is clone of currently opened buffer

name: SPC b N j
desc: create new empty buffer in a new window at the bottom

name: SPC b N k
desc: create new empty buffer in a new window above

name: SPC b N l
desc: create new empty buffer in a new window below

name: SPC b N n
desc: create new empty buffer in current window

name: SPC f A
desc: open a file and replace the current buffer with the new file

name: SPC f c
desc: copy current file to a different location

name: SPC f C d
desc: convert file from unix to dos encoding

name: SPC f C u
desc: convert file from dos to unix encoding

name: SPC f d
desc: delete a file and the associated buffer (ask for confirmation)

name: SPC f E
desc: open a file with elevated privileges (sudo edit)

name: SPC f h
desc: open binary file with hexl (a hex editor)

name: SPC f j
desc: jump to the current buffer file in dired

name: SPC f l
desc: open file literally in fundamental mode

name: SPC f o
desc: open a file using the default external program

name: SPC f t
desc: toggle file tree side bar using Treemacs

name: SPC f v f
desc: add a local variable to the current file

name: SPC f v p
desc: add a local variable to the first line of the current file

name: SPC f y c
desc: show and copy current file absolute path with line and column number in the minibuffer

name: SPC f y d
desc: show and copy current directory absolute path in the minibuffer

name: SPC f y l
desc: show and copy current file absolute path with line number in the minibuffer

name: SPC f y n
desc: show and copy current file name with extension in the minibuffer

name: SPC f y y
desc: show and copy current file absolute path in the minibuffer

name: SPC f e e
desc: open the /.spacemacs.env file where environment variables are set or goes to dotspacemacs/user-env

name: SPC f e C-e
desc: reinitialize the /.spacemacs.env file by importing system and shell environment variables

name: SPC f e v
desc: display and copy the spacemacs version

name: SPC s `
desc: go back to the previous place before jump

name: SPC s s
desc: search with the first found tool

name: SPC s b
desc: search with the first found tool

name: SPC s f
desc: search with the first found tool

name: SPC s w g
desc: Get Google suggestions in emacs. Opens Google results in Browser.

name: SPC s e
desc: edit all occurrences of the current symbol(/)

name: SPC s h
desc: highlight the current symbol and all its occurrence within the current range

name: SPC t h a
desc: toggle automatic highlight of symbol under point after ahs-idle-interval seconds

name: SPC x .
desc: enter the drag stuff transient state

name: SPC x TAB
desc: indent or dedent a region rigidly

name: SPC x a a
desc: align region (or guessed section) using default rules

name: SPC x a c
desc: align current indentation region using default rules

name: SPC x c
desc: count the number of chars/words/lines in the selection region

name: SPC x d l
desc: delete blank lines but one or the single blank line

name: SPC x d SPC
desc: Delete all spaces and tabs around point, leaving one space

name: SPC x g l
desc: set languages used by translate commands

name: SPC x g t
desc: translate current word using Google Translate

name: SPC x i i
desc: cycle symbol naming styles (i to keep cycling)

name: SPC x J
desc: drag down a line of text (enter drag stuff transient state)

name: SPC x K
desc: drag up a line of text (enter drag stuff transient state)

name: SPC x o
desc: use avy to select a link in the frame and open it

name: SPC x t c
desc: swap (transpose) the current character with the previous one

name: SPC x t e
desc: swap (transpose) the current sexp with the previous one

name: SPC x t l
desc: swap (transpose) the current line with the previous one

name: SPC x t p
desc: swap (transpose) the current paragraph with the previous one

name: SPC x t s
desc: swap (transpose) the current sentence with the previous one

name: SPC x t w
desc: swap (transpose) the current word with the previous one

name: SPC x u
desc: set the selected text to lower case

name: SPC x w c
desc: count the number of occurrences per word in the select region

name: SPC x w d
desc: show dictionary entry of word from wordnik.com

name: SPC x y
desc: use avy to copy a link in the frame

name: SPC i u
desc: Search for Unicode characters and insert them into the active buffer.

name: SPC i U 1
desc: insert UUIDv1 (use universal argument to insert with CID format)

name: SPC i U 4
desc: insert UUIDv4 (use universal argument to insert with CID format)

name: SPC i U U
desc: insert UUIDv4 (use universal argument to insert with CID format)

name: SPC z x 0
desc: reset the font size (no scaling) and initiate the font scaling transient state

name: SPC z f 0
desc: reset the frame content size and initiate the frame scaling transient state

name: SPC n f
desc: narrow the buffer to the current function

name: SPC n p
desc: narrow the buffer to the visible page

name: SPC n r
desc: narrow the buffer to the selected text

name: SPC n w
desc: widen, i.e. show the whole buffer again

name: SPC x r /
desc: Explain the regexp around point with rx

name: SPC x r t
desc: Replace regexp around point by the rx form or vice versa

name: SPC x r x
desc: Convert regexp around point in rx form and display the result in the minibuffer

name: SPC x r c
desc: Convert regexp around point to the other form and display the result in the minibuffer

name: SPC x r e p
desc: Convert Emacs Lisp regexp to PCRE

name: SPC x r e t
desc: Replace Emacs Lisp regexp by rx form or vice versa

name: SPC x r e x
desc: Convert Emacs Lisp regexp to rx form

name: SPC x r p e
desc: Convert PCRE regexp to Emacs Lisp

name: SPC k j
desc: go forward to next closing parenthesis

name: SPC k k
desc: go backward to previous opening parenthesis

name: SPC k $
desc: go forward to the end of current sexp

name: SPC k 0
desc: go backward to the beginning of current sexp

name: SPC k I
desc: go back to beginning of current expression and switch to insert state

name: SPC k s
desc: slurp forward: a (bs) c -> a (bs c)

name: SPC k b
desc: barf forward: (a bs c) -> (a bs) c

name: SPC k e
desc: splice, killing forward: (as (bs <point> cs) ds) -> (as bs ds)

name: SPC k v
desc: switch to visual state and begin character-wise selection

name: SPC k C-v
desc: switch to visual state and begin block-wise selection

name: SPC m e $
desc: go to end of line and evaluate last sexp

name: SPC m e c
desc: evaluate current form (a def or a set)

name: SPC m e l
desc: go to end of line and evaluate last sexp

name: SPC m h h
desc: describe elisp thing at point (show documentation)

name: SPC m t q
desc: ask for test function to execute

name: SPC K c c
desc: insert the current value of the macro counter

name: SPC K c f
desc: set the display format for the macro counter

name: SPC K e b
desc: assign a key binding to the last macro

name: SPC K e e
desc: edit last macro in a buffer

name: SPC K e l
desc: edit a macro from lossage information (last 300 executed commands)

name: SPC K e n
desc: give a name to the last macro

name: SPC K e r
desc: write the last macro to a register (use SPC r r to call it)

name: SPC K e s
desc: step by step edit of the last macro

name: SPC K r n
desc: cycle to next macro in ring

name: SPC K r p
desc: cycle to previous macro in ring

name: SPC K r s
desc: swap the first two macros in ring

name: SPC D b 3
desc: ask for 3 opened buffers and start an ediff session with them

name: SPC D b b
desc: ask for 2 opened buffers and start an ediff session with them

name: SPC D b p
desc: ask for a buffer or file that contains a patch to apply to a buffer and start an ediff session with the result

name: SPC D B
desc: ask for a file and run ediff with its backup file

name: SPC D d 3
desc: ask for 3 directories and run ediff on them comparing files that have the same name in all of them

name: SPC D d d
desc: ask for 2 directories and run ediff on them comparing files that have the same name in both

name: SPC D d r
desc: run ediff on a directory comparing its files with their revisions if under version control

name: SPC D f .
desc: start an ediff session between your .spacemacs and its default template in Spacemacs core

name: SPC D f 3
desc: ask for 3 files and start an ediff session with them

name: SPC D f f
desc: ask for 2 files and start an ediff session with them

name: SPC D f p
desc: ask for a buffer or file that contains a patch to apply to a file and start an ediff session with the result

name: SPC D f v
desc: start ediff between versions of a file

name: SPC D m b 3
desc: start an ediff merge session between 2 buffers and their ancestor

name: SPC D m b b
desc: start an ediff merge session between 2 buffers

name: SPC D m d 3
desc: start an ediff merge session between files with the same name in 2 directories and with a 3rd directory containing their ancestor

name: SPC D m d d
desc: start an ediff merge session between files with the same name in 2 directories

name: SPC D m f 3
desc: start an ediff merge session between 2 files and their ancestor

name: SPC D m f f
desc: start an ediff merge session between 2 files

name: SPC D m r 3
desc: start an ediff merge session between two revisions of a file with a common ancestor

name: SPC D m r r
desc: start an ediff merge session between two revisions of a file

name: SPC D r l
desc: start an ediff session between two regions to perform a linewise diff (use this for large regions)

name: SPC D r w
desc: start an ediff session between two regions to perform a wordwise diff (use this for small regions)

name: SPC D w l
desc: compare linewise the portions of visible text of 2 windows which are selected by clicking

name: SPC D w w
desc: compare wordwise the portions of visible text of 2 windows which are selected by clicking

name: SPC p '​
desc: open a shell in project's root (with the shell layer)

name: SPC p t
desc: open Treemacs and add current projectile root to its list of projects

name: SPC p v
desc: open project root in vc-dir or magit

name: SPC r e
desc: show evil yank and named registers

name: SPC e l
desc: toggle the display of the flycheck list of errors/warnings

name: SPC e y
desc: copy each error at cursor position into kill ring

name: SPC e v
desc: verify flycheck setup (useful to debug 3rd party tools configuration)

name: SPC q q
desc: Quit Emacs and kill the server, prompt for changed buffers to save

name: SPC q r
desc: Restart both Emacs and the server, prompting to save any changed buffers

name: SPC q s
desc: Save the buffers, quit Emacs and kill the server

name: SPC g f f
desc: view a file at a specific branch or commit

name: SPC g f m
desc: magit dispatch popup for file operations

name: SPC g H h
desc: highlight regions by age of commits

name: SPC g H t
desc: highlight regions by last updated time

name: SPC g M
desc: display the last commit message of the current line

name: SPC g l c
desc: browse to the current file at a commit

name: SPC g l C
desc: create link to the file at a commit and copy it

name: SPC g l l
desc: browse to file at current lines position

name: SPC g l L
desc: create a link to the file highlighting the selected lines

name: SPC g l p
desc: browse to file at current lines position using permalink

name: SPC g l P
desc: create a link with permalink highlighting selected lines

name: SPC T ~
desc: display tilde in fringe on empty lines
